gauss-linking.gauss-linking::h6g9sfu:
A framed knot is a knot equipped with a smooth choice of normal vector
at every point — equivalently, a ribbon or thin strip tied into a knot. The framing
keeps track of how many times the ribbon twists as it travels along the knot.
gauss-linking.gauss-linking::h1t5gl7u:
RI (twist/untwist) changes the framing by ±1. A framed knot invariant
must therefore be invariant only under RII and RIII, or must account for the twist introduced by RI.
This is why the Kauffman bracket is not a knot invariant until corrected by the writhe.
gauss-linking.gauss-linking::h6089hz:
RII (poke/unpoke) preserves the framing. Two crossings are added or removed simultaneously,
contributing opposite signs that cancel.
gauss-linking.gauss-linking::h12e42ua:
RIII (slide) preserves the framing. No crossings are created or destroyed,
so the total twist is unchanged.
gauss-linking.gauss-linking::h189wav3:
Select a link below and compute its linking number numerically via the Gauss integral.
The heatmap shows the integrand \(\frac{(\mathbf{r}_1 - \mathbf{r}_2) \cdot (d\mathbf{r}_1 \times d\mathbf{r}_2)}{|\mathbf{r}_1 - \mathbf{r}_2|^3}\) as a function of the parameters \((t,s)\).
gauss-linking.gauss-linking::h1gt7p1k:
The degree counts (with sign) how many times the image covers the sphere.
Since degrees are integers, \(\mathrm{Lk}(K,L) \in \mathbb{Z}\).
gauss-linking.gauss-linking::h1ypwylj:
Equivalently, the linking number is the degree of the Gauss map
\(G: T^2 \to S^2\) defined by:

home.home::h1dzqo7l:
For example, the trefoil is the closure of the braid \(\sigma_1^3 \in B_2\), and the
figure-eight knot is the closure of \(\sigma_1 \sigma_2^{-1} \sigma_1 \sigma_2^{-1} \in B_3\).
home.home::h1vaya5y:
The braid group acts naturally on tensor products of quantum group representations.
A representation \(\rho: B_n \to \text{End}(V^{\otimes n})\) that satisfies the
Markov trace property yields a knot invariant. The Jones polynomial arises
from the fundamental representation of the quantum group \(U_q(\mathfrak{sl}(2))\),
and more general quantum group representations produce the colored Jones polynomials
and the full family of Reshetikhin–Turaev invariants.
home.home::h1m7wulx:
The set of all braids on \(n\) strands forms the braid group \(B_n\),
with composition given by stacking braids vertically. The group is generated by
Artin generators \(\sigma_1, \sigma_2, \ldots, \sigma_{n-1}\), where
\(\sigma_i\) crosses strand \(i\) over strand \(i+1\).
home.home::hle0u7a:
This is a powerful invariant that encodes much of the topology of the knot. For example,
the unknot is the only knot whose group is \(\mathbb{Z}\). The trefoil knot group has presentation
\(\langle a, b \mid a^2 = b^3 \rangle\), which is isomorphic to the braid group \(B_3\).
home.home::hyt6p0a:
Jones polynomial skein relation:


$$ q^{-1} V_{L_+}(q) - q\, V_{L_-}(q) = \left(q^{1/2} - q^{-1/2}\right) V_{L_0}(q) $$
